rag_rules: Fix AGREE syntax errors by correcting mismatched input or missing semicolons in annex blocks.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes.
Ensure type consistency between declarations and expressions in AGREE.
Generate AGREE contracts using valid logical operators and proper syntax.
Avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponents in type-level contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence like `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`.
Branching logic in AGREE should use nested `if ... then ... else` expressions.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid identifiers from the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
Do not invent new identifiers or types in AGREE clauses; use only visible ones.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in system implementations
